title: On the Burden of Expectations
author: From: Siddhartha by Hermann Hesse
content: Wisdom cannot be taught. Wisdom which a wise man attempts to teach always sounds like foolishness. One must experience the path to understand the destination.
action: Action: Focus on the 'experience' of a current project rather than just the final deliverable.

title: First Principles Thinking
author: From: Aristotle
content: Break a problem down into its fundamental truths. Rebuild the solution from the ground up, ignoring 'how it's always been done.'
action: Action: Deconstruct a standard process in your field into its core elements and find one redundant step.

title: The Circle of Competence
author: From: Warren Buffett
content: True intelligence is knowing the exact boundary of your understanding. Stay inside the circle, or expand it deliberately.
action: Action: Identify one subject you find interesting but do not fully understand; list the first step to learn it.

title: Optionality
author: From: Nassim Taleb
content: Having many options is the best hedge against an uncertain future. Prioritize flexibility over optimization.
action: Action: Create a 'contingency plan' for your next major decision that allows for a quick exit.

title: The Pygmalion Effect
author: From: Robert Rosenthal
content: Expectations influence outcomes. When we expect higher quality, we unconsciously take the steps necessary to create it.
action: Action: Set a standard for your next task that is 10% higher than your usual baseline.
